reviews.strongestCounterargument: The apparent gain can be manufactured by a taxonomy that already knows where the simulated regression lives; real organizations have noisy, stale risk labels, so the power result may not survive prospective use.
reviews.unsupportedClaimRisk: The weakest initial claim implied that inverse-probability weighting repaired the canary generally; it repairs the population estimand under known nonzero selection probabilities but cannot repair misspecified strata or omitted traffic regions.
reviews.reproductionBarrier: A reader can reproduce the simulation without dependencies, but applying it requires a versioned consequence taxonomy, paired evaluator stability, selection-probability logging, and a held-out incident family that many teams do not yet maintain.
revisions: Added homogeneous, misranked-risk, and no-effect controls and made the misranked result central to the adoption boundary rather than a footnote.
Separated detection and prevalence into two release ledgers with preregistered stop rules, effective-sample requirements, and a population shadow stream.
Added held-out taxonomy validation, case-exposure tracking, and an explicit non-claim for adaptive allocation.

reviews.strongestCounterargument: Provider-published latency and benchmark tables may not transfer to a customer's sensors, thermals, runtime commit, or safety envelope, so a hardware matrix can create false precision despite careful transcription.
reviews.unsupportedClaimRisk: The weakest initial wording treated 15 Hz policy operation and board-specific reasoner timings as one edge-performance story; the revision states that these are different configurations and cannot establish closed-loop safety.
reviews.reproductionBarrier: Full reproduction requires NVIDIA boards, the pinned 4B weights and CUDA runtime, representative physical episodes, DROID-compatible policy plumbing, and safety review; no local hardware run was performed for this analysis.
revisions: Split reasoner, generator, and policy evidence into separate workload contracts and flagged the T2000 resolution mismatch directly in the table and visual.
Added a three-phase offline, hardware-in-the-loop, and shadow-policy rollout with deterministic actuator constraints and concrete rollback triggers.
Added prompt-upsampler dependency, license, non-NVIDIA portability, power, thermal, and p95 unknowns to the adoption boundary.

reviews.strongestCounterargument: The article cannot calculate total cost or comparative accuracy without organization-specific active-committer definitions, Actions pricing, AI usage rates, repository builds, and labeled findings from incumbent analyzers.
reviews.unsupportedClaimRisk: The weakest initial claim implied consolidation would improve quality operations; the revision makes consolidation conditional on supported-language coverage, analyzer parity, actionable precision, reliable builds, and export testing.
reviews.reproductionBarrier: A decision-grade pilot needs billing telemetry, representative repositories, blinded expert labeling, existing analyzer exports, and two normal development cycles; public documentation alone cannot supply those measurements.
revisions: Separated base licenses, deterministic Actions analysis, and metered AI into distinct budget caps and rollout controls instead of a blended per-developer estimate.
Added an observe-only analyzer-parity phase followed by a randomized incremental-AI cohort scored on unique accepted findings, latency, remediation success, and cost.
Added language blind spots, SARIF limits, fail-open versus fail-closed behavior, new-code thresholding, and export/restore rehearsal to the rollback plan.
